title: rudbeckia fulgida var. spathulata
intro: Rudbeckia fulgida var. spathulata, commonly known as Spotted Black-eyed Susan, is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It is traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and fever, due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's roots, flowers, and leaves are used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are believed to offer health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function. Additionally, the plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones, which are known to exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities.
